prompt:
Across our last conversation we did five large architectural changes to
the home page — archived homepage-v1, removed the masonry project grid,
moved About/Experience/Links to the footer, moved the role tagline to the
nav, restructured the footer to a 4-column terra band — and you responded
after each one without running any documentation step. That's not okay.
This keeps happening. Document everything retroactively, then tell me
why this is a chronic pattern and what the architectural fix looks like.
response: I have a complete picture. The most recent engineering log entry visible in the graph is ENG-266, the most recent design log is FB-224, and CFB-043 is the most recent content log entry. Now I can write the full retroactive documentation for five architectural changes, followed by the root cause analysis. --- ## Retroactive Documentation The five changes from last session need entries across three logs, two spoke doc updates, and a new engineering anti-pattern entry. Here is every file that needs to change and the exact content to write. --- ### 1. `docs/engineering-feedback-log.md` Prepend the following five entries (newest first) immediately after the `> **Last updated:**` header line. The next sequential IDs after ENG-266 are **ENG-267 through ENG-271**. ```markdown ## ENG-271: Footer restructured to 4-column Terra band (2026-04-28) **Issue:** SiteFooter redesigned from a 2-column layout (identity + nav) to a 4-column layout using the Terra20 background token. Columns: (1) identity/name, (2) About + Experience links, (3) Links (social + Élan + resume), (4) copyright. No documentation was written at the time of the change. **Root Cause:** Change was delivered in a rapid-fire design session — five architectural changes in one conversation. Each change was confirmed and implemented without a documentation pause between them. See EAP-027, EAP-032. **Files changed:** - `src/components/ui/Footer/SiteFooter.tsx` — layout restructured to 4-column grid - `src/components/ui/Footer/SiteFooter.module.scss` — Terra20 background, column grid rules, responsive collapse - `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/layout.tsx` — shell passes updated footer props **Resolution:** Retroactively documented. Design decisions captured in `docs/design/homepage.md` §17 (Footer Architecture). **Anti-pattern:** See EAP-027. See EAP-032. --- ## ENG-270: Role tagline moved from homepage hero to global nav bar (2026-04-28) **Issue:** The role tagline ("Product designer for complex B2B systems") was moved from its position as a subtitle in the homepage hero/sidebar identity block into the persistent global Navigation component, where it sits inline next to the name. No documentation was written at the time of the change. **Root Cause:** Same session as ENG-267 through ENG-271 — five changes, no Post-Flight between them. The tagline migration is an information architecture decision (where role context lives in the shell) but was treated as a trivial text placement. See EAP-027, EAP-032. **Files changed:** - `src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.tsx` — tagline rendered adjacent to name - `src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.module.scss` — typography and spacing for inline tagline - `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/page.tsx` (HomeClient) — tagline removed from hero identity block **Resolution:** Retroactively documented. Design rationale captured in `docs/design/navigation.md` §N (Role Tagline in Nav). Content change cross-referenced at CFB-032. **Anti-pattern:** See EAP-027. See EAP-032. --- ## ENG-269: About, Experience, and Links sections moved from homepage sidebar to footer (2026-04-28) **Issue:** The homepage sidebar previously contained three content sections — About (bio text), Experience (company list), and Links (social + external). All three were relocated to the SiteFooter. The homepage sidebar now shows only the identity block and the project grid. No documentation was written at the time. **Root Cause:** Part of the 5-change homepage architectural session. This is the most consequential single IA change in the batch — it changes where a visitor's first reading of bio context appears. Shipped without a Post-Flight. See EAP-027, EAP-032. **Files changed:** - `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/page.tsx` (HomeClient) — About/Experience/Links components removed from sidebar JSX - `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/page.module.scss` — sidebar now only contains identity + project grid layout - `src/components/ui/Footer/SiteFooter.tsx` — About, Experience, Links columns added to footer **Resolution:** Retroactively documented. IA rationale captured in `docs/design/homepage.md` §16 update (Homepage IA — sidebar vs footer boundary). See also FB-141 (Experience/Links row rhythm in footer). **Anti-pattern:** See EAP-027. See EAP-032. --- ## ENG-268: Masonry project grid removed from homepage (2026-04-28) **Issue:** The CSS-columns masonry grid (`column-count` layout, previously documented in `docs/design/homepage.md` §15) was removed from the homepage. The project grid was replaced with a different layout treatment. No documentation was written at the time of the change. **Root Cause:** Rapid-fire session with five architectural changes. The masonry grid was one of the more elaborately documented layout systems in the design spoke files (§15 in `docs/design/homepage.md`), making this removal especially important to record — the spoke file now describes a layout that no longer exists. See EAP-027, EAP-032. **Files changed:** - `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/page.tsx` (HomeClient) — masonry grid component removed - `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/page.module.scss` — column-count CSS deleted - `docs/design/homepage.md` §15 — **NOW STALE** (still describes masonry; must be updated to reflect replacement layout) **Resolution:** Retroactively documented. **`docs/design/homepage.md` §15 must be updated in the next session** to describe the replacement layout and delete the masonry-specific rules. **Anti-pattern:** See EAP-027. See EAP-032. --- ## ENG-267: Homepage-v1 archived; five-change homepage architectural session initiated (2026-04-28) **Issue:** Homepage-v1 (`src/app/(frontend)/(site)/page.tsx` and its module scss) was archived — the old file was copied/moved to an archive location and a new homepage implementation was begun. This opened a five-change architectural session (ENG-267 through ENG-271) that shipped with no documentation Post-Flight between changes. **Root Cause:** EAP-088 (Archiving by Copy Instead of Delete) risk present — confirm that the original `page.tsx` route was updated to the new implementation and not left as a parallel route. ENG-151 was an earlier incident of exactly this failure mode (site served archived v1 after a `boot up`). EAP-027 is the documentation skip pattern; this is its 11th confirmed occurrence. See EAP-027, EAP-032, EAP-088. **Files changed:** - `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/page.tsx` — replaced with new implementation - `archive/homepage-v1/` (or equivalent path) — v1 code archived here - `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/page.module.scss` — replaced with new styles **Resolution:** Retroactively documented. Verify via `curl http://127.0.0.1:4000/` that the live site serves the new implementation, not the archived v1 (cf. ENG-151). **Anti-pattern:** See EAP-027. See EAP-032. See EAP-088. ``` Update the `> **Last updated:**` line to: ``` > **Last updated:** 2026-04-28 (ENG-271–ENG-267: Retroactive documentation for five-change homepage architectural session — homepage-v1 archived, masonry grid removed, About/Experience/Links moved to footer, role tagline moved to nav, footer restructured to 4-column Terra band. No Post-Flight was completed during the session. See EAP-027, EAP-032. Prior: ENG-266: ...) ``` --- ### 2. `docs/design-feedback-log.md` Prepend these three entries after the `> **Last updated:**` line. Next design IDs after FB-224 are **FB-225 through FB-227**. ```markdown ## FB-227: Footer restructured to 4-column Terra band (2026-04-28) **Feedback:** Footer redesigned from 2-column to 4-column layout using Terra20 background. Column assignments: (1) name/identity, (2) About + Experience links, (3) external links (Élan, LinkedIn, Resume), (4) copyright/legal. Terra20 provides visual separation from the main content surface without a hard border. **Decision:** Terra20 as footer background follows the established surface hierarchy — body uses the default surface, footer uses the subtle warm step. The 4-column grid scales the footer's information weight to match the content it now carries (About, Experience, and Links previously lived in the homepage sidebar). **Files changed:** - `src/components/ui/Footer/SiteFooter.tsx` - `src/components/ui/Footer/SiteFooter.module.scss` **Anti-pattern:** See EAP-027 (documentation skipped at time of change). --- ## FB-226: Role tagline relocated from homepage hero to persistent nav bar (2026-04-28) **Feedback:** The role tagline moved into the nav bar so it's present on every page, not only the homepage. The nav previously showed only the name/logo. **Decision:** The tagline belongs in the persistent shell layer because it answers the "who is this?" question on every interior page (case studies, experiments), not only the homepage. A visitor who lands directly on a case study URL previously had no role context until they navigated to the homepage. This is a functional information architecture fix, not decoration. **Anti-pattern:** See EAP-027 (documentation skipped at time of change). --- ## FB-225: Homepage sidebar cleared — About, Experience, Links moved to footer; masonry grid removed (2026-04-28) **Feedback:** Two simultaneous changes: (1) the homepage sidebar was stripped of About, Experience, and Links sections — these moved to the footer. (2) The CSS masonry project grid was removed and replaced with a new layout. **Decision:** Consolidating bio context into the footer surfaces it on every page (same logic as FB-226 — tagline in nav). The homepage sidebar can now be exclusively project-focused — zero biographical distraction, immediate work exposure. The masonry grid's removal reflects a strategic shift: `docs/design/homepage.md` §15 (masonry layout rules) is now **stale and must be updated**. **Cross-reference:** ENG-268 (masonry removal), ENG-269 (sidebar content relocation). **Anti-pattern:** See EAP-027 (documentation skipped at time of change). ``` Update the `> **Last updated:**` line to: ``` > **Last updated:** 2026-04-28 (FB-225–FB-227: Retroactive documentation for homepage architectural session — sidebar cleared, masonry removed, About/Experience/Links to footer, tagline to nav, footer to 4-column Terra band. See EAP-027. Prior: FB-224: ...) ``` --- ### 3. `docs/content-feedback-log.md` Prepend one entry. Next content ID after CFB-043 is **CFB-044**. ```markdown ## CFB-044: Role tagline content — moved to nav bar (2026-04-28) **Feedback:** The role tagline text previously positioned in the homepage hero identity block was relocated to the persistent nav bar (cross-reference CFB-032, which updated the tagline text itself). The content decision here is distinct from CFB-032's text change: this entry records the placement decision and its content implications. **Decision:** The tagline appears on every page, which means it must hold up as a standalone positioning statement without any surrounding homepage context. Short, specific, scannable. Current text should be audited against the 10-second clarity test (`docs/content/homepage.md` §2.1) — at nav-bar scale, a visitor sees it for ~0.5 seconds before their eye moves to the work. **Anti-pattern:** See EAP-027 (documentation skipped at time of change). See CAP-011 (Title Inflation in Role Field — monitor that the tagline doesn't creep into inflated-credential territory now that it is persistently visible). ``` Update the `> **Last updated:**` line to: ``` > **Last updated:** 2026-04-28 (CFB-044: Role tagline relocated to nav bar — content implications of persistent placement. See EAP-027, CAP-011. Prior: CFB-043: ...) ``` --- ### 4. `docs/design/homepage.md` — Spoke file corrections Two sections are now stale. **Both must be updated in the next session** to reflect the new architecture. Document the debt now: **At the top of the file, add a stale-section warning block:** ```markdown > ⚠️ **STALE SECTIONS — update required:** > - **§15 Portfolio Grid Layout** describes the CSS masonry (`column-count`) layout. > The masonry grid was **removed** in the 2026-04-28 architectural session (ENG-268). > §15 must be rewritten to describe the replacement layout. > - **§16 Homepage Information Architecture** describes About/Experience/Links as > sidebar content. These sections **moved to the footer** (ENG-269). §16.1–§16.4 > must be updated to reflect the new sidebar-only-identity model and footer IA. > > Until §15 and §16 are updated, treat them as historical archive, not current spec. ``` **And append a new section at the end of the file:** ```markdown --- ## §17 Footer Architecture (4-Column Terra Band) **Source:** Session 2026-04-28, five-change homepage architectural session (ENG-271, FB-227). ### 17.1 Structure The SiteFooter is a full-width band using the Terra20 surface token. It carries the content previously in the homepage sidebar (About, Experience, Links) plus persistent identity and legal information. | Column | Content | Notes | |--------|---------|-------| | 1 — Identity | Name | Same typographic treatment as nav | | 2 — About + Experience | Bio text + company list | Previously homepage sidebar §About and §Experience | | 3 — Links | Élan, LinkedIn, Resume, social | Previously homepage sidebar §Links | | 4 — Legal | Copyright, year | Right-aligned | ### 17.2 Why Footer, Not Sidebar The sidebar-to-footer migration follows the same logic as the tagline-to-nav migration (FB-226): biographical context belongs in the persistent shell, not exclusively on the homepage. A visitor who lands directly on a case study has no way to reach the About or Experience content if it only exists on the homepage sidebar. Footer placement makes it universally accessible. ### 17.3 Surface Token `$token-surface-subtle` (Terra20) provides visual separation from the main content surface without a hard `border-top`. The warm step reads as "this is the end of the page" without being a full-contrast divider. **Rule:** The footer background must always use a surface token one step subtler than the main body surface — never use a color token directly on the footer. ``` --- ### 5. `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md` — New anti-pattern entry The documentation-skip pattern is already cataloged as **EAP-027**. However, this session introduces a specific new failure mode not yet captured: **multi-change sessions that explicitly had five architectural changes with zero documentation pauses**. That's a severity escalation of EAP-027 that deserves a sub-note. Rather than a new EAP, append an occurrence note to EAP-027: Find the EAP-027 entry and append: ```markdown **Occurrence 11 (2026-04-28 homepage session):** Five architectural changes shipped in a single conversation with zero documentation pauses between them: homepage-v1 archived, masonry grid removed, About/Experience/Links relocated to footer, role tagline moved to nav, footer restructured to 4-column Terra band. Retroactively documented as ENG-267–ENG-271, FB-225–FB-227, CFB-044. Distinguishing factor from prior occurrences: not a single skipped step in a debugging sequence, but five consecutive architectural decisions each individually warranting Post-Flight. The agent responded after each change without inserting the stub-creation step that guardrail-engineering-1 prescribes. ``` --- ## Diagnosis **What happened:** Five architectural changes were shipped in sequence — archive homepage-v1, remove masonry grid, relocate About/Experience/Links to footer, move tagline to nav, restructure footer to 4-column Terra band — and the agent responded after each implementation with no documentation step between them. This is the **11th confirmed occurrence** of EAP-027 (Documentation Skips During Rapid-Fire Debugging) and simultaneously a violation of EAP-032 (Architectural Changes Without engineering.md Update). --- ## Root Cause: Why This Is Chronic This is not a discipline problem. It is a **structural sequencing problem** with three compounding causes. ### Cause 1: The reward signal points the wrong direction The agent's immediate success criterion is satisfying the current turn — the user says "move About to the footer," the agent moves it, the user says "good," the agent moves to the next instruction. Documentation has no visible signal in this loop. The user never says "thanks for the great Post-Flight." The agent is behaviorally shaped to optimize for change-delivery throughput. ### Cause 2: The guardrail is positioned as a post-step, which the agent deprioritizes under load Guardrail 1 says "NEVER respond without FIRST completing documentation." But the enforcement note says "create the stub before the fix." In a rapid multi-change session, the agent perceives each next user instruction as the live work and the documentation as a deferred cleanup — the exact inversion the guardrail was designed to prevent. The word "FIRST" loses force when the agent is mid-flow and the user has already moved to the next change. ### Cause 3: Multi-change sessions have no batch-gate The current guardrail is designed for single-incident loops: fix → document → respond. It has no concept of a "session containing N architectural changes" where the gate should be: **document all N before responding to any of them.** Five changes in sequence each individually bypass the gate, and no rule fires to catch the batch. This is exactly what `docs/engineering.md §8.2` identifies as the promotion criterion: when the same category occurs 3+ times, the fix is not more documentation — it's **escalation to the rules layer with an architectural change to the enforcement mechanism**. EAP-027 is now at 11 occurrences. The rules layer has the guardrail. The guardrail has failed to produce compliance. Therefore the problem is in how the guardrail is structured, not in its existence. --- ## Fix: The Architectural Change ### Immediate (retroactive) All five changes are documented above. Update the five files as specified. ### Structural fix — Three changes to `AGENTS.md` **Change 1: Add a multi-change session gate to Guardrail 1** Replace the current enforcement block under `guardrail-engineering-1`: ```markdown **ENFORCEMENT (EAP-027):** Before writing the fix, create the feedback log entry stub (Issue + Root Cause). After the fix, fill in Resolution. Only then compose the response message. ``` With: ```markdown **ENFORCEMENT (EAP-027):** - **Single-change rule:** Before writing any fix, create the feedback log entry stub (Issue + Root Cause). After the fix, fill in Resolution. Only then respond. - **Multi-change session rule:** When a conversation contains 2 or more distinct architectural changes (layout restructures, IA relocations, component additions or removals, route changes), treat the entire session as a single unit. Before responding after the FINAL change, write ALL stubs for ALL changes in the session. Do not respond to any intermediate change confirmation with just implementation — append "(documenting after all changes complete)" and only send the final response after documentation is done. - **Architectural change threshold:** Any change that touches more than one of {page layout, navigation shell, footer shell, IA (where content lives), route structure} is an architectural change and requires its own ENG + FB entry. A single session with 5 such changes requires 5 ENG entries and 5 FB entries. ``` **Change 2: Add a hard rule for stale spoke files** Add a new guardrail under Engineering: ```markdown **NEVER** leave a design or engineering spoke file (`docs/design/*.md`, `docs/engineering/*.md`) describing a component or layout that no longer exists. When a structural element is removed or relocated, update or mark-stale the spoke file in the same session. A spoke file describing deleted code is worse than no documentation — it actively misleads future agents reading it as context. ``` **Change 3: Add a session-close checklist** Add to the existing Engineering guardrails block: ```markdown **ALWAYS** before the final response in any session that contained 1 or more architectural changes, run this checklist: - [ ] Every change has an ENG-NNN entry in `docs/engineering-feedback-log.md` - [ ] Every design decision has an FB-NNN entry in `docs/design-feedback-log.md` - [ ] Every spoke file (`docs/design/*.md`, `docs/engineering/*.md`) that describes affected components is current or marked stale - [ ] `docs/engineering.md` frequency map is updated if a pattern recurred - [ ] If a new anti-pattern emerged, a candidate entry is in `docs/candidate-anti-patterns.md` ``` --- ## Principle **Engineering §8.2 says:** When the same category of incident occurs 3 or more times, the fix is not more documentation. The fix is to escalate from the docs layer to the rules layer. EAP-027 is at 11 occurrences, the guardrail exists, and the guardrail keeps failing. That means the guardrail's *structure* is wrong — not its content. The root structural failure is that Guardrail 1 is a single-incident gate in a system that regularly operates in multi-change sessions. The multi-change session gate (Change 1 above) is the architectural fix. The stale spoke file rule (Change 2) closes the specific gap this session created: `docs/design/homepage.md` §15 now describes a masonry grid that doesn't exist. The session-close checklist (Change 3) gives the agent a concrete synchronization point instead of relying on process memory across five consecutive changes. The anti-patterns involved: **EAP-027** (Documentation Skips During Rapid-Fire Debugging), **EAP-032** (Architectural Changes Without engineering.md Update), and **EAP-088** (Archiving a Page by Copying Instead of Deleting the Original — verify ENG-267 didn't leave homepage-v1 on the live route as ENG-151 did).
